fastSteps: Identify variables: \lambda = 5 \times 10^{-7} \text{ m}, D = 1 \text{ m}, d = 5 \times 10^{-4} \text{ m}
examTricks: Convert all units to SI (meters) before calculating to avoid decimal errors.

learnMode.keyIdea: Fringe width represents the periodic spatial distance between consecutive intensity maxima on a screen, determined by the ratio of wavelength to slit angular separation.

steps.title: Define Fringe Width
steps.reasoning: The fringe width is the physical separation between adjacent bright or dark fringes. This depends on the ratio of wavelength to the slit separation angle.

steps.title: Unit Normalization
steps.reasoning: Ensure all physical parameters are in standard SI units (meters).

steps.title: Final Calculation
steps.reasoning: Substitute the normalized values into the fringe width equation to solve for distance.

learnMode.takeaway: Remember that fringe width is directly proportional to wavelength and screen distance, and inversely proportional to slit separation.

assumptions.assumption: Small angle approximation (sin θ ≈ tan θ ≈ θ)
assumptions.validity: Valid when screen distance D is much larger than slit separation d.
assumptions.failure: Fails if the screen is placed very close to the slits, where wavefronts are not effectively parallel.

commonMistakes: Forgetting to convert mm to m for the slit separation.
Confusing the separation of the central maximum with fringe width.
learnMode.importantObservation: The fringe width is constant for a given experimental setup, resulting in a uniform fringe pattern.
